identificationRemarks: most commonly found around farmland, usually cape hares don't like areas with too much shrubby or tree-like vegetation, they like to see around and they don't like dodging bushes and trees while fleeing... scrub hares are the opposite, they like cover around them (cover can be in the form of knee high Seriphium bushes towering above heavily grazed grass, so no need to look for woodlands)... I see one if the nature pockets has woody vegetation and that is more than enough cover for saxatilis, who probably ventures into the open to feed occasionally... I think you could get away with saxatilis, I can't see much that says it is capensis so it is very likely saxatilis

occurrenceRemarks:
Dropped bunny - Montagu WCape


A bunny carcass on top of a bush. The munch pattern suggests that this was a rooikat kill. The location suggests it was stolen by a bird and dropped. I have watched male black eagles try to impress a lady by flying around dangling various very dead things.

The interesting thing is that the ears have dried up to about a tenth of live size. Bunny ears must be made of water.
